articles.summary: In this live webinar with Metronome [4], Martin Casado, General Partner at Andreessen Horowitz, will share how both startups and public companies are shifting from subscriptions [4] to results-based, hybrid monetization for AI.

articles.title: Register for the live session on November 4
RESILIENCY AND SCALE
articles.summary: Our systems, optimized for efficiency and scale, have become fragile. Whether in data, manufacturing, or information, the drive for lower costs and centralization has sacrificed resiliency. Rebuilding it will require expensive shifts toward redundancy and local control.

articles.title: THE RIGHT TO BEAR TECHNOLOGY
articles.summary: The Second Amendment is America's original right to technology, the tool that enforces our freedoms. From Cold War missile labs to modern Silicon Valley, US innovation has always balanced ideals with strength. Our tech sector must remember its duty to protect both.

articles.title: YOUR PRICING IS (PROBABLY) BROKEN
articles.summary: Most leaders know their pricing isn't perfect, and that's normal. Every model has tradeoffs, so the goal isn't to overhaul it but to adjust it. Add clear tiers, smart upsells, and flexible levers that better match your product's value and growth story.

articles.title: PRIORITIZATION STARTS WITH STRATEGIC PRIORITIZATION
articles.summary: Prioritization is strategy. Every ranking of value or urgency reflects a choice about where to build or defend advantage. By mapping benefits, drivers, and enduring “powers,” teams can focus less on quick wins and more on investments that compound over time.

articles.title: DON'T SACRIFICE AGILITY FOR STABILITY
articles.summary: Most payment platforms make you choose: agility and innovation or

articles.title: scale and reliability. With Marqeta, launch payments experiences
articles.summary: that perform at scale and grow with your business. From optimizing spend and cash flow to building seamless, rewards-driven customer experiences, Marqeta's solutions are designed for longevity. See what

articles.title: BEYOND RELEASE MANAGEMENT: FEATURE FLAGS FOR PRODUCT DISCOVERY
articles.summary: Feature flags can accelerate learning, not just control rollouts. Teams can turn guesswork into fast, confident product decisions by using painted doors to test demand, dogfooding to refine value, and targeted betas to validate functionality.

articles.title: ONLY 100 METRICS MATTER
articles.summary: Businesses drown in data, but only a small core truly drives understanding: about 100 metrics, 50 key events, and 150 defining attributes. When these are structured through clear equations and semantic links, teams replace noise with insight, and AI becomes a reasoning partner, not just a reporting tool.

articles.title: WHEN YOUR METRICS START MANAGING YOU
articles.summary: Teams fail when they chase metrics instead of meaning. The piece offers five principles for measuring what matters: start with outcomes, balance multiple metrics, favor leading over lagging indicators, add qualitative context, and design dashboards that reveal insight instead of confirming bias.

articles.title: HOW TO MASTER GOOGLE AI TOOLS: COMPLETE MASTERCLASS WITH JACLYN KONZELMANN (32 MINUTE PODCAST)
articles.summary: Google's AI product teams prototype with Imagen, Veo, and Opal to test ideas in minutes rather than weeks. Jaclyn Konzelmann's frameworks teach PMs to think big, launch fast, and evolve constantly, focusing on creativity, adaptability, and building in public instead of chasing perfection.

articles.title: KNOW WHEN AI AGENTS ARE USING YOUR PRODUCT
articles.summary: AI agents are already acting on user accounts. Agent Shield [14]shows you which sessions are human, bot, or agent—helping PMs uncover risks in minutes, for free.

articles.title: THERE IS NO GOD TIER VIDEO MODEL
articles.summary: Specialize by use case, streamline workflows, and build on the diverse strengths of today's video models.

articles.title: HOW TO CONNECT NORTH STAR METRICS AND OKRS
articles.summary: Real progress happens when the North Star Metric, Product Strategy, and OKRs align so every short-term goal supports a clear strategic direction.
